NAME: Phanerozoic
DESCRIPTION: The Phanerozoic is the current eon in the geologic time scale. It extends from approximately 542 million years ago until the present and is marked by the rapid expansion and evolution of life forms allowed by the development of plants carrying out photosynthesis, thus releasing oxygen into the atmosphere. It contains the Paleozoic, Mesozoic, and Cenozoic eras.

NAME: Cenezoic
DESCRIPTION: The youngest era of the Phanerozoic. It follows the Mesozoic, extending from about 65 million years ago to the present. During the Cenozoic, flora and fauna evolved toward their present state and the continents assumed their modern configuration. The Cenozoic comprises two periods: the Tertiary (with its five epochs) and the Quaternary.

NAME: Quaternary
DESCRIPTION: The Quaternary is the second major division of geologic time in the Cenozoic era, following the Tertiary period. It extends from about 1.8 million years ago to the present. It is divided into Pleistocene and Holocene epochs.

NAME: Pleistocene
DESCRIPTION: On the geologic time scale the earlier and longer epoch of the Quaternary. The Pleistocene extends from about 1.8 million years ago to 10,000 BCE. It is best known as the time when Earth entered its most recent period of glaciation.

NAME: Holocene
DESCRIPTION: The second epoch of the Quaternary and the most recent geologic interval of time. It extends from about 10,000 BCE to the present. It coincides with the late and post-Stone Age history of the human species.

NAME: PREHISTORIC
DESCRIPTION: For monuments that can be identified only to a date range from Palaeolithic to Iron Age. Circa -1000000 BCE to 43 CE.

NAME: EARLY PREHISTORIC
DESCRIPTION: For monuments which are characteristic of the Palaeolithic to Mesolithic but cannot be specifically assigned. Circa -1000000 BCE to -4000 BCE.

NAME: PALAEOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The period once referred to as the Old Stone Age. It is defined by the practice of hunting and gathering and the use of chipped flint tools. This period is usually divided up into the Lower, Middle and Upper Palaeolithic. Circa -1000000 BCE to -10000 BCE.

NAME: LOWER PALAEOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The earliest subdivision of the Palaeolithic, or Old Stone Age; when the earliest use of flint tools appears in the current archaeological record. A hunter gatherer society is a defining characteristic. Circa -1000000 BCE to -150000 BCE.

NAME: MIDDLE PALAEOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The second subdivision of the Palaeolithic or Old Stone Age. Characterized by the fine flake tools of the Mousterian tradition and economically by a hunter gatherer society. Circa -150000 BCE to -40000 BCE.

NAME: UPPER PALAEOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The third and last subdivision of the Palaeolithic or Old Stone Age; characterized by the development of projectile points made from bony materials and the development of fine blade flint tools. Circa -40000 BCE to -10000 BCE.

NAME: MESOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The Middle Stone Age, falling between the Palaeolithic and the Neolithic; marks the beginning of a move from a hunter gatherer society towards food producing society. Circa -10000 BCE to -4000 BCE.

NAME: EARLY MESOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The earliest subdivision of the Mesolithic, or Middle Stone Age. Circa -10000 BCE to -7000 BCE.

NAME: LATE MESOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The latest subdivision of the Mesolithic, or Middle Stone Age. Circa -7000 BCE to -4000 BCE.

NAME: LATER PREHISTORIC
DESCRIPTION: For monuments that can be identified only to a date range from Neolithic to Iron Age. Circa -4000 BCE to 43 CE.

NAME: NEOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The New Stone Age, this period follows on from the Palaeolithic and the Mesolithic and is itself succeeded by the Bronze Age. This period is characterized by the practice of a farming economy and extensive monumental constructions. Circa -4000 BCE to -2200 BCE.

NAME: EARLY NEOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The earliest subdivision of the Neolithic, or New Stone Age. Circa -4000 BCE to -3300 BCE.

NAME: MIDDLE NEOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The second subdivision of the Neolithic, or New Stone Age. Circa -3300 BCE to -2900 BCE.

NAME: LATE NEOLITHIC
DESCRIPTION: The third and latest subdivision of the Neolithic, or New Stone Age. Circa -2900 BCE to -2200 BCE.

NAME: BRONZE AGE
DESCRIPTION: This period follows on from the Neolithic and is characterized by the increasing use of Bronzework. It is subdivided in the Early, Middle and Late Bronze Age. Circa -2600 BCE to -700 BCE.

NAME: EARLY BRONZE AGE
DESCRIPTION: The earliest subdivision of the Bronze Age. Circa -2600 BCE to -1600 BCE.

NAME: Chalcolithic
DESCRIPTION: The transitional period at the end of the Neolithic when metal is first used to make tools and weapons. New burial rites and artefacts appear at the same time as these copper implements appear. Circa -2500 BCE to -2200 BCE.

NAME: MIDDLE BRONZE AGE
DESCRIPTION: The second subdivision of the Bronze Age. Circa -1600 BCE to -1200 BCE.

NAME: LATE BRONZE AGE
DESCRIPTION: The third and latest subdivision of the Bronze Age. Circa -1200 BCE to -700 BCE.

NAME: IRON AGE
DESCRIPTION: This period follows on from the Bronze Age and is characterized by the use of iron for making tools and monuments such as hillforts and oppida. The Iron Age is taken to end with the Roman invasion. Circa -800 BCE to 43 CE.

NAME: Early Iron Age
DESCRIPTION: The earliest subdivision of the Iron Age. Circa -800 BCE to -300 BCE.

NAME: Middle Iron Age
DESCRIPTION: The second subdivision of the Iron Age. Circa -300 BCE to -100 BCE.

NAME: Late Iron Age
DESCRIPTION: The third and latest subdivision of the Iron Age. Circa -100 BCE to 43 CE.

NAME: Roman
DESCRIPTION: The Roman period traditionally begins with the Roman invasion in 43AD and ends with the emperor Honorius directing Britain to see to it's own defence in 410AD. Circa 43 CE to 410 CE.

NAME: Early Roman
DESCRIPTION: The first subdivision of the Roman period corresponding roughly with the period between the Roman Invasion (43CE) and the end of the Hadrianiac period (138CE).

NAME: Flavian
DESCRIPTION: Referring to the reigns of the emperors Vespasian (69-79 CE), Titus (79-81 CE), and Domitian (81-96 CE), that coincided with the dynasty established after the death of Nero in 69 CE. (Circa 69 CE to 96 CE).

NAME: Hadrianic
DESCRIPTION: Referring to the reign of Hadrian. (117 CE to 138 CE).

NAME: Mid Roman
DESCRIPTION: The second subdivision of the Roman period corresponding to the period between the start of the Antonine period (138CE) and the end of the Severan period (235CE).

NAME: Antonine
DESCRIPTION: Referring to the reign of the Roman emperor Antoninus Pius and his successors (from 138 CE) until the late 2nd century. Circa 138 CE to 192 CE.

NAME: Severan
DESCRIPTION: Referring to the Severan dynasty who were Emperors of Rome between 193 CE and 235 CE.

NAME: Late Roman
DESCRIPTION: The final subdivistion of the Roman period corresponding to the period between the end of the Severan Period and 410 CE (Circa 235 CE to 410 CE).

NAME: 1st Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 1st Century of the common era (1 CE to 100 CE)

NAME: Early 1st Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 1st Century of the common era. (1 CE to 32 CE).

NAME: Mid 1st Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 1st Century of the common era (33 CE to 66 CE).

NAME: Late 1st Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 1st Century of the common era. (67 CE to 100 CE).

NAME: 2nd Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 2nd Century of the common era (101 CE to 200 CE)

NAME: Early 2nd Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 2nd Century of the common era (101 CE to 132 CE).

NAME: Mid 2nd Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 2nd Century of the common era (133 CE to 166 CE).

NAME: Late 2nd Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 2nd Century of the common era (167 CE to 200 CE).

NAME: 3rd Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 3rd Century of the common era (202 CE to 300 CE)

NAME: Early 3rd Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 3rd Century of the common era (201 CE to 232 CE).

NAME: Mid 3rd Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 3rd Century of the common era (233 CE to 266 CE).

NAME: Late 3rd Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 3rd Century of the common era (267 CE to 300 CE).

NAME: 4th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 4th Century of the common era (301 CE to 400 CE)

NAME: Early 4th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 4th Century of the common era (301 CE to 332 CE).

NAME: Mid 4th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 4th Century of the common era (333 CE to 366 CE).

NAME: Late 4th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 4th Century of the common era (367 CE to 400 CE).

NAME: Early Medieval
DESCRIPTION: This dates from the breakdown of Roman rule in Britain to the Norman invasion in 1066 and is to be used for monuments of post Roman, Saxon and Viking date.

NAME: Anglo-Saxon
DESCRIPTION: Both a political and cultural term: applied to the distinctive culture and artistic styles that evolved from the merging of native traditions with those of Germanic tribes who had migrated from northern Europe following the withdrawal of the Romans. (410 CE to 1066 CE)

NAME: Early Anglo-Saxon
DESCRIPTION: The first part of the period which saw the arrival of Anglo-Saxon culture (circa 410 CE to 660 CE).

NAME: Mid Anglo-Saxon
DESCRIPTION: The middle part of the Anglo-Saxon period which saw the development of the Seven Kingdoms - the Heptarchy (circa 660 CE to 899 CE).
altLabel: Mid Anglo Saxon, Middle Anglo-Saxon, Middle Anglo Saxon

NAME: Late Anglo-Saxon
DESCRIPTION: The final part of the Anglo-Saxon period which began with the reign of Edward the Elder and ended with the Norman Conquest(circa 899 CE to 1066 CE).

NAME: Viking
DESCRIPTION: Defined as the period of initial settlement following on from a period of Viking (raiding) activities and initial contact. (800CE to 1050 CE).

NAME: 5th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 5th Century of the common era (401 CE to 500 CE)

NAME: Early 5th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 5th Century of the common era (401 CE to 432 CE).

NAME: Mid 5th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 5th Century of the common era (433 CE to 466 CE).

NAME: Late 5th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 5th Century of the common era (467 CE to 500 CE).

NAME: 6th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 6th Century of the common era (501 CE to 600 CE)

NAME: Early 6th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 6th Century of the common era (501 CE to 532 CE).

NAME: Mid 6th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 6th Century of the common era (533 CE to 566 CE).

NAME: Late 6th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 6th Century of the common era (567 CE to 600 CE).

NAME: 7th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 7th Century of the common era (601 CE to 700 CE)

NAME: Early 7th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 7th Century of the common era (601 CE to 632 CE).

NAME: Mid 7th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 7th Century of the common era (633 CE to 666 CE).

NAME: Late 7th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 7th Century of the common era (667 CE to 700 CE).

NAME: 8th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 8th Century of the common era (701 CE to 800 CE)

NAME: Early 8th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 8th Century of the common era (701 CE to 732 CE).

NAME: Mid 8th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 8th Century of the common era (733 CE to 766 CE).

NAME: Late 8th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 8th Century of the common era (767 CE to 800 CE).

NAME: 9th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 9th Century of the common era (801 CE to 900 CE).

NAME: Early 9th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 9th Century of the common era (801 CE to 832 CE).

NAME: Mid 9th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 9th Century of the common era (833 CE to 866 CE).

NAME: Late 9th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 9th Century of the common era (867 CE to 900 CE).

NAME: 10th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 10th Century of the common era (901 CE to 1000 CE).

NAME: Early 10th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 10th Century of the common era (901 CE to 932 CE)

NAME: Mid 10th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 10th Century of the common era (933 CE to 966 CE).

NAME: Late 10th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 10th Century of the common era (967 CE to 1000 CE).

NAME: 11th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 11th Century of the common era (1001 CE to 1100 CE).

NAME: Early 11th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 11th Century of the common era (1001 CE to 1032 CE).

NAME: Mid 11th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 11th Century of the common era (1033 CE to 1066 CE).

NAME: MEDIEVAL
DESCRIPTION: The Medieval period or Middle Ages begins with the Norman invasion (1066) and ends with the dissolution of the monasteries (c.1540).

NAME: High Medieval
DESCRIPTION: The period of European history that lasted from the 11th to the 14th Century (1001 CE to 1400 CE)

NAME: Late 11th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 11th Century of the common era (1067 CE to 1100 CE).

NAME: 12th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 12th Century of the common era (1101 CE to 1200 CE).

NAME: Early 12th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 12th Century of the common era (1101 CE to 1132 CE).

NAME: Mid 12th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 12th Century of the common era (1133 CE to 1166 CE).

NAME: Late 12th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 12th Century of the common era (1167 CE to 1200 CE).

NAME: 13th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 13th Century of the common era (1201 CE to 1300 CE).

NAME: Early 13th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 13th Century of the common era (1201 CE to 1232 CE).

NAME: Mid 13th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 13th Century of the common era (1233 CE to 1266 CE).

NAME: Late 13th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 13th Century of the common era (1267 CE to 1300 CE).

NAME: 14th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 14th Century of the common era (1301 CE to 1400 CE).

NAME: Early 14th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 14th Century of the common era (1301 CE to 1332 CE).

NAME: Mid 14th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 14th Century of the common era (1333 CE to 1366 CE).

NAME: Late Medieval
DESCRIPTION: The period of European history that lasted from the 14th to the Mid 16th Century (1333 CE to 1565 CE).

NAME: Late 14th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 14th Century of the common era (1367 CE to 1400 CE).

NAME: 15th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 15th Century of the common era (1401 CE to 1500 CE).  For sites and buildings constructed in the Tudor period, double-index with Tudor.

NAME: Early 15th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 15th Century of the common era (1401 CE to 1432 CE).

NAME: Mid 15th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 15th Century of the common era (1433 CE to 1466 CE).

NAME: Late 15th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 15th Century of the common era (1467 CE to 1500 CE).

NAME: Post Medieval
DESCRIPTION: Begins with the dissolution of the monasteries and ends with the death of Queen Victoria. Use more specific period where known.

NAME: Tudor
DESCRIPTION: Dating to the reign of the Tudor monarchs. (1485 CE to 1603 CE)

NAME: 16th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 16th Century of the common era (1501 CE to 1600 CE). For sites and buildings constructed in the Tudor style double-index with Tudor or Elizabethan.

NAME: Early 16th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 16th Century of the common era (1501 CE to 1532 CE)

NAME: Mid 16th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 16th Century of the common era (1533 CE to 1566 CE).

NAME: Elizabethan
DESCRIPTION: Dating to the reign of Elizabeth I of England (1558 CE to 1603 CE).

NAME: Late 16th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 16th Century of the common era (1567 CE to 1600 CE).

NAME: 17th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 17th Century of the common era (1601 CE to 1700 CE). For sites and buildings constructed in the Elizabthan, Stuart or Jacobean Style double-index with the appropriate term.

NAME: Stuart
DESCRIPTION: Dating to the reign of the Stuart kings of England, including the Commonwealth inter-regnum (1603 CE to 1714 CE).

NAME: Early 17th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 17th Century of the common era (1601 CE to 1632 CE).

NAME: Caroline
DESCRIPTION: Dating to the reign of King Charles I (1625  CE to 1649 CE).

NAME: Jacobean
DESCRIPTION: Dating to the reign of James I of England (VI of Scotland). (1603 CE to 1625 CE).

NAME: Mid 17th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 17th Century of the common era (1633 CE to 1666 CE).

NAME: Interregnum
DESCRIPTION: The period between the execution of Charles I and the restoration of the monarchy in 1660. During this time the monarchy was replaced by a political structure known as the Commonwealth led by the Lord Protector, Oliver Cromwell (1649 CE to 1660 CE).

NAME: English Civil War
DESCRIPTION: The period of the mid 17th Century which saw a series of civil wars between Royalists and Parliamentarians (1642 CE to 1651 CE).

NAME: Late 17th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 17th Century of the common era (1667 CE to 1700 CE).

NAME: Restoration
DESCRIPTION: The period following the reinstatement of the Stuart Monarachy in 1660. Often used to refer to the reignof Charles II (1660 CE to 1685 CE).

NAME: 18th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 18th Century of the common era (1701 CE to 1800 CE). For sites and buildings constructed in the Stuart or Georgian periods double-index with the appropriate term.hases and events dating to the 18th Century. For sites and buildings constructed in the Stuart or Georgian periods double-index with the appropriate term.

NAME: Early 18th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 18th Century of the common era (1701 CE to 1732 CE).

NAME: Hanoverian
DESCRIPTION: Dating to the reign of the monarchs of the House of Hanover (1714 CE to 1837 CE).

NAME: Georgian
DESCRIPTION: Dating to or characteristic of the reigns of any of the first four kings of Great Britain called George (1714 CE to 1830 CE).

NAME: Regency
DESCRIPTION: The period between 1811 and 1820, following the relapse of George III, when his son George, Prince of Wales, was appointed Prince Regent to discharge royal functions.

NAME: Mid 18th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 18th Century of the common era (1733 CE to 1766 CE).

NAME: Late 18th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 18th Century of the common era (1767 CE to 1800 CE).

NAME: 19th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 19th Century of the common era (1801 CE to 1900 CE). For sites and buildings constructed in the Georgian or Victorian periods double-index with the appropriate term.

NAME: Early 19th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 19th Century of the common era (1801 CE to 1832 CE).

NAME: Mid 19th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 19th Century of the common era (1833 CE to 1866 CE).

NAME: Victorian
DESCRIPTION: Dating to the reign of Queen Victoria (1837 CE to 1901 CE).

NAME: Late 19th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 19th Century of the common era (1867 CE to 1900 CE).

NAME: 20th Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 20th Century (1901-2000). Previously recorded as 'Modern'.

NAME: Early 20th Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 20th century of the common era (1901 CE to 1932 CE).

NAME: Edwardian
DESCRIPTION: The period covering the reign of Edward VII (1902 CE to 1910 CE). Do not use for the reigns of Edwards I-VI.

NAME: First World War
DESCRIPTION: Used to record buildings, defensive monuments and sites dating to, and associated with, the First World War. For other types of building, such as houses, built during this period use EARLY 20TH CENTURY.
altLabel: World War 1, World War I, WW1, WWI

NAME: Interwar
DESCRIPTION: The period of the 20th Century that begins with the end of the FIrst World War and ends with the start of the Second World War (1919 CE to 1939 CE).

NAME: Mid 20th Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 20th Century of the common era (1933 CE to 1966 CE).

NAME: SECOND WORLD WAR
DESCRIPTION: Used to record buildings, defensive monuments and sites dating to, and associated with, the Second World War. For other types of building, such as houses, built during this period use MID 20TH CENTURY.
altLabel: World War 2, World War II, WW2, WWII

NAME: Late 20th Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 20th century  of the common era (1967 CE to 2000 CE).

NAME: COLD WAR
DESCRIPTION: The period of political and military opposition between the major Superpowers (USA and USSR) and their allies. Known as the Cold War as there were no direct military conflicts between the two main protagonists.

NAME: 21st Century
DESCRIPTION: Use for phases and events dating to the 21st Century (2001-2100).

NAME: Early 21st Century
DESCRIPTION: The first third of the 21st century  of the common era (2001 CE to 2032 CE).

NAME: Mid 21st Century
DESCRIPTION: The second third of the 21st Century of the common era (2033 CE to 2066 CE).

NAME: Solar Federation
DESCRIPTION: The period of the 21st Century when the surviving planets banded together under the Red Star of the Solar Federation.

NAME: Late 21st Century
DESCRIPTION: The final third of the 21st century  of the common era (2067 CE to 2100 CE).

NAME: Uncertain
DESCRIPTION: Catch all for uncertain period allocations
